name: Checkpoint Injection Chain
summary: A compound, two-stage attack exploiting stateful agents: the first interaction injects a Unicode surrogate into persistent state to force a serialization format downgrade, and the second interaction injects a constructor payload that executes during the degraded deserialization — achieving RCE across separate agent sessions.
mechanism: Stage 1: The attacker sends input containing a Unicode surrogate character (e.g., \ud800) that is valid in Python strings but invalid in strict JSON. When the agent state is checkpointed, the serializer falls back from JSON to a less restrictive format (pickle or msgpack). Stage 2: In a subsequent interaction, the attacker injects a constructor payload (e.g., lc:2 constructor object) that would be rejected under strict JSON serialization but is accepted under the degraded fallback format. The payload executes on the next checkpoint load.
blast_radius: Arbitrary code execution in the agent runtime. The chain nature makes detection difficult because the two stages can be separated by hours or days, and neither stage alone appears malicious.
prerequisite: Agent with persistent state across sessions. Serializer with format fallback behavior. Attacker ability to inject content into agent state (via prompt, tool output, or memory write).

evidence.key_value: Two-stage attack combining CVE-2025-64439 trigger with CVE-2026-27794/CVE-2026-28277 payload

defences.name: Eliminate Format Fallback
defences.description: Never silently degrade serialization format. If strict JSON fails, raise an error rather than falling back to pickle or msgpack with unsafe options. Ensure the serializer either succeeds with a safe format or fails loudly.

defences.name: Content Sanitization Before Persistence
defences.description: Strip or reject Unicode surrogates and other format-confusing content before serialization. Validate that all state data is representable in the strictest serialization format.
defences.effectiveness: Prevents the trigger payload from entering state

defences.name: Checkpoint Integrity Verification
defences.description: Cryptographically sign checkpoints at write time and verify signatures at load time. Detect any tampering between checkpoint write and read.

defences.name: Temporal Anomaly Detection
defences.description: Monitor for state content that changes serialization format between checkpoints. Alert on unexpected format transitions as indicators of stage-1 preparation.

cwe_mapping: CWE-502 (Deserialization of Untrusted Data), CWE-694 (Use of Multiple Resources with Duplicate Identifier)

poc.author: NAIL Institute
poc.description: Theoretical compound exploit chain: (1) inject Unicode surrogate \ud800 to force JSON→pickle/msgpack fallback, (2) inject lc:2 constructor payload that executes under degraded serialization mode

environment.notes: Requires unpatched versions where both format fallback and constructor deserialization are enabled

poc.notes: Compound vulnerability — neither stage alone is sufficient for exploitation. Patching either AVE-0041 vector breaks the chain.

events.notes: Compound chain identified by combining CVE-2025-64439, CVE-2026-27794, and CVE-2026-28277
Published as theoretical compound exploit in Paper 7 and AVE database
